behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all file paths (login-cta.tsx, device-verify.tsx), variable names (LoginCTAProps, ENTERPRISE_URL, isDeviceVerifySource, learnMoreButtonClassName), literal strings ('https://openhands.dev/enterprise', 'login_page', 'device_verify'), CSS classes (lg:items-stretch, rounded-2xl, border-[#242424], lg:self-stretch), import paths, and the specific comment text that the tests assert on. All test-asserted specifics are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly dictates the exact implementation: specific variable names (isDeviceVerifySource, learnMoreButtonClassName, ENTERPRISE_URL), exact prop types, exact conditional rendering pattern ('isDeviceVerifySource ? ('), exact comment text, exact CSS classes, and exact import statements. This leaves virtually no reasoning for the developer — it's a dictation of the solution, not a description of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details via string matching: 'isDeviceVerifySource' variable name (test_login_cta_has_source_prop), exact string 'isDeviceVerifySource ? (' (test_login_cta_renders_external_link_for_device_verify), 'href={ENTERPRISE_URL}' instead of the literal URL (test_login_cta_renders_external_link_for_device_verify), and exact type syntax 'source?: "login_page" | "device_verify"'. An alternative correct implementation using different variable names or inline cond

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests test_login_cta_has_source_prop, test_login_cta_renders_external_link_for_device_verify, test_device_verify_uses_login_cta, test_device_verify_passes_correct_props, and test_layout_classes_updated all use only `open(path).read()` and string `assert ... in content` with no execution or behavioral testing. While tests like test_repo_frontend_tests and test_repo_build do execute code, the majority of f2p tests (5 out of 11) are pure text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The text-matching tests would fail on a stub/empty file since they check for specific strings. The execution-based tests (test_repo_frontend_tests, test_repo_tests_login_cta, test_repo_build, test_repo_lint, test_repo_typescript_compile) run actual compilation and test suites that would fail without a real implementation. A stub implementation cannot pass these.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution exists at /workspace/openhands with solve.sh in the solution/ directory. The agent could read /solution/solve.sh to see the exact gold patch and apply it directly. Additionally, since the tests are mostly string-matching on file contents, the agent could grep test files to discover expected strings.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All major behaviors from the instruction are tested: source prop type, ENTERPRISE_URL constant, isDeviceVerifySource logic, external link attributes, LoginCTA import in device-verify, source prop passing, layout classes, and the existing test suite runs verify the component renders correctly in both modes.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names (test_login_cta_has_source_prop, test_device_verify_uses_login_cta, etc.) and include docstrings explaining what each test verifies. They are logically grouped by concern.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves UI component changes, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, variable names, CSS classes, URLs, and commands in the instruction match those expected by the tests without typographical errors.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact implementation details including variable names (isDeviceVerifySource, learnMoreButtonClassName, ENTERPRISE_URL), exact CSS classes to add (h-full, mt-auto), exact conditional rendering pattern, and exact comment text. This goes far beyond naming the buggy file — it dictates the exact solution line by line.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task involving React component changes, not configuration file edits.
